title: A Roman Wedding
description: Claudia prepares for her wedding day and the celebrations that follow.

latin_text.text: Claudia, puella Rōmāna, nūptūra erat. māter eam diu ōrnābat.
Claudia vestem albam gerēbat et corōnam flōrum in capite portābat.
sponsus nōmine Gaius domum advēnit cum amīcīs suīs.
sacerdōs verba sacra dīxit et signa fausta cēpit. omnēs gaudēbant.
post caerimōniam convīvium magnum erat. cibus et vīnum aderat.
noctū spōnsus Claudiam ad domum novam dūxit. amīcī cantābant et

vocabulary_notes.meaning: was about to get married (future participle of nūbō)

model_translation.text: Claudia, a Roman girl, was about to get married. Her mother had been dressing her for a long time. Claudia was wearing a white dress and carrying a crown of flowers on her head. Her fiancé named Gaius arrived at the house with his friends.

A priest spoke the sacred words and took the favourable omens. Everyone was rejoicing. After the ceremony there was a great feast. There was food and wine. During the night the husband led Claudia to the new home. Friends were singing and laughing. 'Good luck!' they shouted.

key_phrases: carrying a crown of flowers on her head
arrived at the house with his friends
led Claudia to the new home
